interfacesProduced: CommandDescriptor Rust struct (structured argv and shell invocation dispatch)
ExecutionRecord struct (PID, start time, output reference)
Shell execution async API (spawn, stream stdout/stderr, cancel) over Unix socket
SIGTERM/SIGKILL cancellation protocol with 5s grace period
15-minute default timeout enforcement
interfacesConsumed: VSH-003 ActionDescriptor/TierDecision firewall classify() contract
~/.vashion/policy/exec-allowlist.json allowlist config
tokio::process (external Rust crate)
stories.rationale: technicalNotes explicitly states 'VSH-003 (Policy Firewall) is the admission gate — VSH-001 must call VSH-003 before spawning any process.'

interfacesProduced: DockerClient Rust struct wrapping bollard or docker CLI
Docker operation enum (list, start, stop, build, inspect, logs)
ActionDescriptor construction for Docker operations
Streaming output contract (identical to VSH-001)
Destructive op (prune, rm) Tier 2 classification rule
interfacesConsumed: VSH-003 ActionDescriptor/TierDecision firewall classify() contract
VSH-003 plan_token approval contract for Tier 2 ops
bollard Rust crate or docker CLI (external)
stories.rationale: technicalNotes explicitly states 'Depends on VSH-003 being scaffolded first' and 'All operations use same Tier plan_token contract as VSH-001.'

interfacesProduced: ActionDescriptor Rust struct with 8 required fields
TierDecision enum/struct (Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3)
plan_token lifecycle API (issue on Tier 1/2, resolve on approval, reject on Tier 3)
AutoApprovalPolicy struct with decision_hash + nonce + TTL (default 30s)
/approvals outbound webhook to Brain (Bearer token auth, 3-retry backoff)
Audit log write contract (TierDecision written before execution)
interfacesConsumed: ~/.vashion/policy/allowlist.json (external user-editable config)
stories.rationale: Phase 1 foundation story; technicalNotes identifies it as 'the critical shared gate used by VSH-001, VSH-002, and VSH-004' with no upstream story dependencies named.

interfacesProduced: File operation API (read, write, delete, move, list) over Unix socket
Workspace root config reader from ~/.vashion/config.toml
Path canonicalization and symlink escape detection function
Per-operation tier assignment rules (delete always Tier 2)
interfacesConsumed: VSH-003 ActionDescriptor/TierDecision firewall classify() contract
~/.vashion/config.toml workspace roots config (external)
stories.rationale: implementationPlan explicitly states 'Wire all operations through VSH-003 firewall' and acceptance criteria requires TierDecision contract for every file operation.

interfacesProduced: Session token struct { token, epoch, issued_at, ttl } written to ~/.vashion/auth/core.token (mode 600)
Token validation API (check token AND epoch on every Unix socket connection)
Rekey protocol (epoch increment, Brain degraded-state transition, token reload)
Encrypted credential store API (Anthropic/OpenAI/Ollama) with [REDACTED] log filter
ModelRegistry struct (name, provider, context_window, cost_tier)
Auth state struct for inclusion in VSH-007 checkpoint
interfacesConsumed: ~/.vashion/auth/core.token file path (external filesystem)
stories.rationale: No other story's interface is required; auth token generation and credential store are standalone Phase 1 capabilities with no named upstream story dependency in technicalNotes or implementationPlan.

interfacesProduced: Heartbeat snapshot event schema (processes, containers, disk, memory, network)
DeltaClassification enum (routine / notable / urgent)
Escalation state machine per resource (normal → warning → urgent → sustained_urgent)
Brain /events webhook delivery with Bearer token auth and 3-retry backoff
24h pulse history store with automatic pruning
interfacesConsumed: VSH-005 session token for Bearer token webhook authentication
tokio interval scheduler (external Rust crate)
stories.rationale: implementationPlan requires 'Bearer token auth' on Brain webhook delivery; VSH-005 is the sole producer of session tokens in the architecture, but VSH-005 is not explicitly named in VSH-006's text.

interfacesProduced: Checkpoint struct (active processes, open Docker ops, pending Tier 2 approvals, auth state reference, heartbeat cursor)
Atomic checkpoint write API (write to temp, then rename) triggered on SIGTERM/SIGINT and pre-Tier-2
Checkpoint rotation scheme (keep last 5 at ~/.vashion/checkpoints/)
Clean/dirty classification API on startup
interfacesConsumed: VSH-005 auth state struct (for checkpoint inclusion)
VSH-006 urgent event delivery API (dirty checkpoint surfaced as urgent event)
stories.rationale: implementationPlan explicitly names both: 'Integrate auth state reference from VSH-005 into checkpoint struct' and 'surface dirty as urgent event via VSH-006.'

interfacesProduced: SQLite schema at ~/.vashion/memory.db in WAL mode (episodic table, durable table, approval_state table)
Memory Unix socket API (read, write, query, promote, expire operations)
Single async write queue (tokio channel) with core|brain source tagging
Context budget enforcement (item-level drop, never truncation)
approval_state table for pending plan_token records
interfacesConsumed: VSH-003 plan_token schema (shapes approval_state table for pending Tier 2 records)
stories.rationale: Acceptance criteria states 'Pending Tier 2 plan_token records stored in MEM_S; never silently expire,' implying the approval_state schema must reflect VSH-003's plan_token contract, though VSH-003 is not explicitly named in VSH-008's implementationPlan.

interfacesProduced: MCP tool definitions (shell_exec, docker_op, file_read, file_write, memory_query, heartbeat_status)
Unix socket client with session token + epoch auth and 30s per-call timeout
LLM fallback chain (Anthropic → OpenAI → Ollama) with cost logging (model, tokens in, tokens out, duration ms)
Three failure mode handlers (connection refused → degraded, auth rejection → rekey, timeout → degraded)
traceparent propagation contract across all hops
interfacesConsumed: VSH-001 shell execution Unix socket API
VSH-002 Docker operation Unix socket API
VSH-004 file operation Unix socket API
VSH-005 session token + epoch auth contract
VSH-005 ModelRegistry struct for LLM fallback chain
VSH-006 heartbeat_status Unix socket API
VSH-007 checkpoint state (recovery path)
VSH-008 memory Unix socket API (memory_query, write, promote, expire)
stories.rationale: technicalNotes explicitly states 'Depends on Phase 1 (VSH-001 through VSH-008) complete'; each Phase 1 story produces a Unix socket API or auth contract that VSH-009 wraps as a named MCP tool.

interfacesProduced: Goal priority queue API (urgent/operator/scheduled tiers with preemption semantics)

VSH-009 traceparent propagation contract
Soul.md and Behavior.md (external files, hard error if missing)

interfacesProduced: Relevance scoring model with tunable weights from ~/.vashion/memory-config.toml

Durable lifecycle API (immutable writes, deprecation chain for updates, soft/hard delete)

VSH-008 MEM_S context budget limits (via VSH-009)

request_id (UUID v4) generation and traceparent injection at request entry
